meaning: n.犹太宗教仪式所用的烛台之一种
examples: Ellie dug up the candles, lit them, said the blessings, placed the menorah on her window sill and spent the rest of the evening in her room studying.
She really didn't want to light her menorah either.
I saw that menorah in your window and I started to cry.
Later, as the cleanup crew dug through the rubble, they discovered this menorah, caked in dirt and mold.
Like the Kopelmans, they have a Christmas tree with dreidel ornaments and a menorah, which they light each night.
